scenarioA.title: Post-Mortem: checkout-service Latency Incident
scenarioA.executiveSummary: A deployment of checkout-service v2.4.1 introduced an N+1 database query pattern that caused p99 latency to spike from 450ms to 2.3s. The SRE Agent identified the root cause in 20 minutes. The service was rolled back to v2.4.0, resolving the issue within 33 minutes of detection.

timeline.event: PagerDuty page sent to Sarah K.
Root cause confirmed: N+1 query in v2.4.1

scenarioA.rootCause: Deployment v2.4.1 introduced an N+1 query in the checkout flow. The ORM change in commit abc123 removed eager loading for order_items. Under production load (>500 req/s), this caused database query count to increase 47x.
scenarioA.resolution: Rolled back to v2.4.0 via ArgoCD at 15:02 UTC. Latency normalized within 6 minutes. Error rate returned to baseline. SLO burn rate < 1x.
wentWell: SRE Agent identified root cause in 20 minutes
Rollback was clean and fast (6 min to normalize)
War room was efficient (3 people, clear roles)
wentWrong: N+1 query was not caught in code review
No load test in staging for this code path
ORM change review checklist doesn't include eager loading check

actionItems.action: Add eager loading lint rule to CI
Add load test for checkout flow
Create alert for query count spike

scenarioB.title: Post-Mortem: api-gateway SLO Burn Rate Breach
scenarioB.executiveSummary: An expired TLS certificate on auth-service caused api-gateway to return 502 errors starting at 03:14 UTC. This consumed 62% of the monthly SLO error budget. The SRE Agent identified the root cause within 34 minutes and flagged a matching memory from 90 days ago. Certificate was rotated after a war room was convened.

timeline.event: PagerDuty page sent to Mike T.
Memory match: INV-0623 (90 days ago, same cert issue)
Root cause confirmed: TLS cert expiry

detection.howDetected: NR SLO burn rate alert (>10x threshold)
detection.timeToRootCause: 34 min (SRE Agent + memory match + human confirmation)

scenarioB.rootCause: TLS certificate on auth-service expired at 03:14 UTC after 90-day validity period. Certificate was not auto-rotated. This is the 2nd occurrence in 90 days (previous: INV-0623 on Jan 9).
scenarioB.resolution: Certificate rotated via manual process (cert-manager was not configured for auto-renewal). Rolling restart of auth-service pods completed at 06:30 UTC.
wentWell: SRE Agent matched memory from 90 days ago, accelerating diagnosis
War room was organized quickly with clear roles
Mike T. confirmed cert issue within 30 minutes of page
wentWrong: cert-manager auto-renewal was never configured after INV-0623
No monitoring for certificate expiry dates
Action items from INV-0623 post-mortem were not completed
Resolution took 6+ hours due to manual cert process

actionItems.action: Configure cert-manager for auto-renewal on all services
Add cert expiry monitoring alert (30-day warning)
Audit all TLS certs across prod for expiry dates
